node-red.name: Node-RED
node-red.short_description: Node-RED is a free, open-source, browser-based visual tool for wiring together hardware, APIs, and online services.

features.description: A browser-based drag-and-drop editor for wiring together flows that automate tasks.
features.title: Visual flow editor

features.description: A large library of community-built nodes and flows extends what Node-RED can connect to.
features.title: 5,000+ community nodes

features.description: Built on Node.js with a non-blocking, event-driven architecture.
features.title: Event-driven Node.js runtime

features.description: Runs on low-cost hardware such as Raspberry Pi, BeagleBone Black, and Arduino, as well as Android.
features.title: Edge and IoT deployment

features.description: Can be deployed locally via Docker/Node.js, or run on FlowFuse, AWS, or Microsoft Azure.
features.title: Cloud deployment options

pros: Completely free and open source
Large ecosystem of 5,000+ community nodes and flows
Runs on inexpensive hardware for home automation and IoT edge use
Governed by the OpenJS Foundation with active project leadership
Mature project in active development since 2013
cons: No official Node-RED-run cloud hosting; commercial hosting is provided by third-party sponsor FlowFuse
Visual flow-based programming can become harder to manage for very complex logic
Community nodes vary in quality and maintenance since they are contributed by third parties

faqs.answer: Yes, Node-RED is free and open source, distributed via npm.
faqs.question: Is Node-RED free?

faqs.answer: Node-RED was created in early 2013 by Nick O'Leary and Dave Conway-Jones of IBM's Emerging Technology Services group, and they continue leading the project today.
faqs.question: Who created Node-RED?

faqs.answer: Node-RED is governed by the OpenJS Foundation, with FlowFuse as its current active sponsor.
faqs.question: What organization governs Node-RED?

faqs.answer: Node-RED runs on Docker and standard Node.js installations, as well as low-cost devices such as Raspberry Pi, BeagleBone Black, and Arduino, plus Android.
faqs.question: What hardware can run Node-RED?
